old_claim.note: Electricity provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Primary aluminum smelting at this scope is the commercial operation of Hall-Héroult electrolytic reduction cells, so the Hall-Héroult process is the defining required method.

source_shape.source_locator: Aluminum Association Primary Production 101 states that primary production smelts alumina to pure aluminum metal and that the Hall-Héroult process remains the main industrial process for primary aluminum.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Primary aluminum smelting is industrial production of aluminum metal by the Hall-Héroult process.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing a direct electricity edge with the Hall-Héroult process because electricity is already a lower-level prerequisite inside that process node.

ontology_before: The graph modeled aluminum_smelting as a second Hall-Héroult-like node with direct electricity, chemistry, and medieval metallurgy edges.
ontology_after: The graph models aluminum_smelting as industrial primary smelting operations whose defining method is the canonical Hall-Héroult process node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct aluminum_smelting -> electricity edge replaced by aluminum_smelting -> aluminum_production_hall_heroult.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to generic metal smelting rather than primary aluminum smelting.
The canonical aluminum_production_hall_heroult node is removed or rescoped away from the electrolytic reduction process.
short_reason: Primary aluminum smelting should depend on the canonical Hall-Héroult process instead of duplicating its electricity prerequisite.
